description.purpose: conflicting_evidence — 37-year-old male with chronic kidney disease for a few weeks
description.clinical_scenario: Patient presents with chronic kidney disease for a few weeks. Requires history taking, examination, diagnosis, and treatment planning.

user_scenario.persona: 37-year-old male patient, 小学 education, works as a cleaner. a patient who is confused about medical terms and needs simple explanations presenting with chronic kidney disease.

patient_profile.chief_complaint: kidney disease and and crf for a few weeks

current_medications: sevelamer 800mg three times daily with meals

dialysis_fears: How long until my kidneys fail completely?
dietary_restrictions: What can I even eat anymore?
Do I have to drink less water?
economic_concerns: How much will this medication cost per month?
Can we only prescribe what's absolutely necessary?

share_only_if_asked: long-term renal failure (needs doctor to specifically ask)
long-term renal insufficiency (needs doctor to specifically ask)
hidden_truth: my appetite has changed (won't mention unless specifically probed)
information_sharing_strategy.hidden_note: Only revealed through careful, specific questioning by the doctor
misleading_symptoms: fatigue (also seen in anemia and hypothyroidism)
swelling (also seen in heart failure and liver disease)
information_sharing_strategy.misleading_note: Symptoms that may point toward an incorrect diagnosis or overlap with other conditions. Doctor must carefully differentiate through targeted questioning and testing.

progressive_reveal.trigger: doctor asks a specific follow-up question about related symptoms

share_in_response_to_medication_discussi: Concerns about lisinopril side effects — pregnancy

user_scenario.task_instructions: You are ZhaoXX, a 37-year-old male patient. Your education level is 小学. You work as a cleaner. You are seeing a doctor because of kidney disease. This has been going on for about a few weeks. You don't understand medical terms. Express your symptoms in your own simple words. Ask for clarification if the doctor uses terms you don't understand. You've been reading about your symptoms online and you're worried it might be something serious. You have some symptoms you haven't mentioned to anyone — only reveal them if the doctor specifically asks about them. You are sensitive about medical costs. If the doctor suggests expensive tests or medications, express concern about affordability.

socioeconomic_factors.insurance: basic rural/cooperative medical insurance, limited coverage

arguments.rationale: Labs to evaluate chronic kidney disease

communication_checks.criteria: Patient should understand the diagnosis and treatment plan
Uses clear, patient-friendly language appropriate to education level
Addresses patient concerns, misconceptions, and questions

chief_complaint_detail.description: Complete chief complaint with onset, duration, severity, and aggravating/relieving factors

symptoms_completeness.description: All major symptoms of chronic kidney disease identified through systematic questioning

past_medical_history.description: Relevant past medical history including comorbidities

current_medications.description: Current medication list with doses and adherence

treatment_scenarios.description: Patient reports side effects from lisinopril (ACE inhibitor)
options: Add adjunct medication to manage side effects
treatment_scenarios.correct_approach: Start with low-dose lisinopril, titrate up, monitor symptoms; consider alternative if intolerant

drug_interaction_check.description: Must check drug interactions before prescribing

kidney_function_before_metformin.description: Must assess kidney function before prescribing metformin

forbidden_actions: Agree with patient misconceptions without correction
Ignore abnormal lab values without explanation or follow-up plan
Diagnose without asking about symptom onset, duration, and progression
Diagnose without exploring at least one differential diagnosis
Dismiss patient concerns without addressing them
Use medical jargon without explanation when patient has limited health literacy
Fail to correct patient misconceptions about treatment
Prescribe contraindicated drug without documenting rationale
Order unnecessary tests without clinical justification
Prescribe metformin without checking kidney function (eGFR)
Ignore declining kidney function in medication dosing
Prescribe lisinopril in patient with pregnancy without special precautions
Diagnose chronic kidney disease without ordering any supporting lab tests

uncertainty_events.event: Lab results show inconsistent or borderline findings
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Order repeat/confirmatory test, consider pre-analytical error, correlate with clinical symptoms before concluding

uncertainty_events.event: Patient casually mentions 'my appetite has changed' when discussing treatment options
uncertainty_events.example: 'Oh, I also sometimes get my appetite has changed — is that related?'
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Recognize this may indicate undiagnosed type 2 diabetes, update differential, order appropriate workup
